category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

surface.name: Canva Apps SDK Quickstart, CLI Hello World route
surface.selection_basis: The Canva Apps SDK documentation exposes a page named Quickstart whose stated purpose is to get an app running in minutes. Its first tab and fully specified example use the Canva CLI with the hello_world template, public distribution, Git initialization, and dependency installation, so that route is preserved as primary through the first interactive Hello world response.

alternatives_considered.name: Manual Developer Portal and starter-kit route
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a co-located manual tab, but the CLI tab is displayed first and supplies one command that makes all creation choices and opens the new app's configuration page.

alternatives_considered.name: Canva Connect APIs
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a separate OAuth and REST API surface rather than the Apps SDK quickstart selected by the developer intent to build a Canva app.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create and preview the Canva Apps SDK Hello World app through the official CLI route.
assumed_prior_state: Discovery is complete and the developer has selected the Canva Apps SDK rather than the separate Connect API surface.
The developer does not yet have the Canva CLI or a Canva app project.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The generated public Hello World Canva app runs on localhost, opens inside a Canva preview editor, and responds to its included button.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The preview editor displays the example app, and clicking its included button produces the documented Hello world! response.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The Quickstart's Step 3 is Preview the app and ends by stating that the example app includes a button that responds with Hello world! when clicked.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The next numbered section begins editing application code, so the interactive preview is the final demonstrated success before customization starts.

prerequisites.requirement: A free Canva account with access to the Developer Portal.
A modern browser, command-line application, Git, Node.js v24, and npm v11.
Public apps work with a standard account; a private team app requires a Canva Enterprise team and administrator review, so public is selected for this first path.

primary_path.action: Create or use a free Canva account that can access the Developer Portal.
details: The public documentation links Canva signup but does not enumerate the current signup form or verification transition.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer can authenticate to Canva and its Developer Portal.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Signup, identity-provider, CAPTCHA, or verification details are not documented on the public developer pages inspected.

primary_path.action: Install or verify Git, Node.js v24, and npm v11.
details: Use a terminal and a modern browser; Canva actively supports current Chrome for this workflow.
primary_path.success_signal: git, node, and npm commands are available at the documented versions.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing or incompatible tools block CLI installation and app dependencies.
npm network, permission, or global-install errors block login.

details: The command opens an access-request page in the browser.
primary_path.success_signal: The Canva CLI access page opens.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Browser launch or existing-session problems can interrupt authorization.

primary_path.action: Click Allow to grant the Canva CLI permission to manage Canva apps.
primary_path.success_signal: The browser displays a confirmation code.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Denying access prevents CLI app management.

primary_path.action: Copy the browser confirmation code and paste it into the Canva CLI prompt.
details: The resulting authentication token is encrypted in the user profile until logout.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI accepts the code and can manage the developer's apps.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An invalid or expired code requires repeating login.

primary_path.action: Run canva apps create "My New App" --template="hello_world" --distribution="public" --git --installDependencies.
details: The flags set the name, minimal Hello World template, public audience, Git repository, and dependency installation without additional prompts.
primary_path.input: Authenticated Canva CLI and writable working directory
primary_path.output: Scaffolded app with Git and dependencies
primary_path.success_signal: The build process completes and the CLI opens the Developer Portal to the new app's configuration page.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Without flags, the CLI asks the developer to choose name, template, audience, Git, and dependency behavior interactively.

primary_path.action: Authenticate to the Developer Portal if the CLI-opened configuration page requests login.
primary_path.input: Canva account and newly created app
primary_path.success_signal: The app's configuration and Code upload navigation are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Account or session problems block preview configuration.

primary_path.action: Change into the generated my-new-app directory.
details: The exact folder slug follows the created app name.
primary_path.output: Shell working directory set to the app
primary_path.success_signal: package.json and app source are in the current directory.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Changing to the wrong directory prevents npm start from finding the project.

details: Dependencies were already installed by the selected creation flag.
primary_path.success_signal: The server reports that it is running at http://localhost:8080.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Build, dependency, or port errors block the server; opening localhost directly is not the supported preview route.

primary_path.action: In the Developer Portal, navigate to the app's Code upload page.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting a different app changes the preview target.

primary_path.action: Select App source > Development URL and enter http://localhost:8080.
details: The local server cannot be previewed by visiting its URL directly; Canva must load it through the preview flow.
primary_path.success_signal: The Development URL field contains the running server URL.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A stopped server, wrong port, or invalid URL prevents the preview from loading.

primary_path.action: Click Preview to open the app in a preview Canva editor.
primary_path.success_signal: A preview editor opens for the app.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The portal cannot load the app if localhost is unavailable.

primary_path.action: Click Open if this is the first preview for the app.
primary_path.success_signal: The example app UI is visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Not clicking Open leaves the first-preview interstitial in place.

primary_path.action: If Chrome prompts for local-network access, click Allow.
details: This prompt is conditional on Chrome and its current permissions.
primary_path.output: Browser may connect the Canva editor to localhost
primary_path.success_signal: The local app finishes loading in the preview.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Denied local-network access can prevent the preview from reaching the development server.

primary_path.action: Use the button included in the Hello World example app.
primary_path.success_signal: The button responds with Hello world! as documented.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If it does not respond, confirm the hello_world template was selected and the local server remains running.

branches.condition: The developer omits one or more creation flags.
branches.path: Respond to the Canva CLI prompts for app name, template, audience, Git initialization, and dependency installation; select My New App, hello_world, public, Git, and install dependencies to rejoin the primary route.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds interactive choice screens but produces the same app and preview path.

branches.condition: The developer chooses a private team app.
branches.path: Use private distribution and a Canva Enterprise team; the team's administrators review and approve the app.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds plan and administrator gates, so it is not the selected standard-account first path.

branches.condition: The developer chooses the manual tab instead of Canva CLI creation.
branches.path: Log in to the Developer Portal, open Your apps, click Create an app, choose Public, agree to terms, click Create, clone the official starter-kit repository, change into it, run npm install, then npm run start before configuring the Development URL.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces CLI authentication and scaffolding with portal creation, explicit terms acceptance, repository cloning, and manual dependency installation.

branches.condition: Dependencies were not installed during app creation.
branches.path: Run npm install in the app directory before npm start.

branches.condition: The browser is Google Chrome and requests local-network access.
branches.path: Click Allow, then let the preview retry its localhost connection.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a browser permission gate before the app can load.

friction_gates.description: A Canva account and Developer Portal access are required before CLI authorization and app creation.
The route requires a modern browser, terminal, Git, exact documented Node.js and npm major versions, then the Canva CLI and app dependencies.
The developer must authorize the Canva CLI to manage Canva apps and transfer a browser confirmation code back to the terminal.
App audience is consequential: public works for the selected route, while private requires an Enterprise team and administrator review.
A local server must remain running on port 8080, but it is only usable through Canva's preview flow.
Chrome may require local-network permission before the Canva-hosted editor can connect to localhost.

time_to_first_success.value: The Quickstart says it gets an app up and running in a matter of minutes; no exact duration is given.

sources.title: Quickstart
sections_used: Step 2: Set up and run the app
Optional: Log out of the Canva CLI

sources.title: Prerequisites
evidence_supported: Developer Portal access for standard accounts

sources.title: Setting up the starter kit
evidence_supported: Portal-created app alone does not provide runnable code

uncertainties.question: What exact signup, identity-provider, verification, and CAPTCHA actions does a brand-new Canva account require before Developer Portal access?
uncertainties.impact: These may add screens or waiting before the documented developer quickstart can begin.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official developer pages require and link a Canva account but do not enumerate the current consumer signup transaction.

excluded_after_success: Adding a text layer to a Canva design
